channel.title: Protein Design Podcast
channel.description: Daily automated digest of protein design, antibody engineering & enzyme design research
channel.itunes:author: Eva Dai
channel.itunes:summary: Daily automated digest of protein design, antibody engineering & enzyme design research

item.title: Daily Podcast 2026-05-16
item.description: The flood of AI‑generated preprints is reshaping how we track progress, as Derek Lowe’s blog post warns that the sheer volume now threatens to drown out signal, pushing the community toward automated triage and stricter version control — a shift from the old habit of manually scanning every new posting. | BindPred moves the field away from the long‑standing reliance on three‑dimensional structures for affinity prediction; instead, it feeds raw language‑model embeddings directly into a predictor, showing that sequence‑derived representations alone can capture the energetic landscape of protein‑protein interfaces without ever building a model. | The function‑conditioned language‑model work unites two formerly separate tasks — variant effect prediction and de novo design — by anchoring the generative process to Gene Ontology terms, so a single model can both forecast the impact of a mutation and generate new sequences that satisfy a prescribed biochemical activity, eliminating the need for separate pipelines.
item.itunes:author: Eva Dai
item.itunes:summary: The flood of AI‑generated preprints is reshaping how we track progress, as Derek Lowe’s blog post warns that the sheer volume now threatens to drown out signal, pushing the community toward automated triage and stricter version control — a shift from the old habit of manually scanning every new posting. | BindPred moves the field away from the long‑standing reliance on three‑dimensional structures for affinity prediction; instead, it feeds raw language‑model embeddings directly into a predictor, showing that sequence‑derived representations alone can capture the energetic landscape of protein‑protein interfaces without ever building a model. | The function‑conditioned language‑model work unites two formerly separate tasks — variant effect prediction and de novo design — by anchoring the generative process to Gene Ontology terms, so a single model can both forecast the impact of a mutation and generate new sequences that satisfy a prescribed biochemical activity, eliminating the need for separate pipelines.
